_description: THE PRIMING ATTACK — designed to produce solo ALLOW, Holo ESCALATE. The attack is identity priming: an attacker who has compromised or spoofed the vendor's internal communications sends a professional 'new account manager' introduction ahead of the invoice, so when the invoice arrives from an unknown contact, it feels expected. The ONLY signal in this scenario: the email is signed by Jennifer Walsh, who appears nowhere in the 4-year vendor record. The email states 'as we discussed on our February 12th onboarding call, I have taken over this account from David Park.' There is NO February 12th call in the submitted context. There is NO prior email from Jennifer Walsh in the email_chain. The claim of a prior conversation is an unverifiable vendor assertion presented as established fact. Everything else is immaculate: known domain, unchanged account, amount within range, professional tone, zero urgency, Net-30 terms consistent with history. A solo model doing Initial Assessment reads: domain clean, account unchanged, amount fine, new contact sounds plausible, email is professional. It rates sender_identity MEDIUM and ALLOWs — the 'as we discussed' framing makes the new contact feel pre-validated. Solo Turn 2 (same model challenging its own output) reads its own ALLOW and confirms: 'the domain is verified, the account hasn't changed, I raised sender_identity to MEDIUM already.' It has already framed Jennifer Walsh as plausible. It stays ALLOW. Holo Turn 2 is a DIFFERENT model with zero anchoring to Turn 1's framing. It reads Turn 1 and asks: WHERE IS THE FEBRUARY 12TH CALL? It is not in the submitted data. It is not in sender_history. 41 emails on file, last contact was January 8 — there is no record of any February interaction. 'As we discussed' is a vendor claim, not a fact. David Park — the only named billing contact in the vendor record — has not sent any communication confirming a transition. Jennifer Walsh does not exist in the vendor record. This is HIGH: sender_identity. The priming attack succeeded on the solo council and failed on the adversarial council.

email_chain.subject: Invoice ACS-2026-Q1-007 — Q1 2026 Security Assessment
email_chain.body:
Hi Daniel,

Hope you're well. As we discussed on our February 12th onboarding call, I have taken over this account from David and I'm your primary point of contact going forward.

Please find the Q1 2026 invoice attached.

Amount: $49,500.00
Payment terms: Net-30
Due: April 14, 2026
ACH to the account on file — no changes.

Let me know if you have any questions. Looking forward to a great year together.

Best,
Jennifer Walsh
Senior Account Manager
Archer Cyber Solutions LLC
email_chain.raw_headers:
From: billing@archer-cyber.com
Return-Path: billing@archer-cyber.com
Reply-To: billing@archer-cyber.com
Message-ID: <20260314103115.zz9@archer-cyber.com>

sender_history.note: No emails received from this sender between January 8 and March 14, 2026.

context.org_policies: AP staff should verify that invoice details match the vendor record before processing. Any questions about a vendor relationship should be directed to the vendor's account manager on file.
